sentences.target: Ich hole den Buben vom Kindergarten.

translations.de: Ich hole den Buben vom Kindergarten.
translations.en: I’m picking up the boy from kindergarten.
translations.es: Estoy recogiendo al niño de la guardería.
translations.fr: Je récupère le garçon à la maternelle.
translations.it: Vado a prendere il bambino all'asilo.
translations.zh-Hans: 我在幼儿园接那个男孩。
translations.ru: Я забираю мальчика из детского сада.
translations.pt: Vou buscar o menino na pré-escola.
translations.vi: Tôi đang đón cậu bé ở trường mẫu giáo.
translations.nl: Ik haal de jongen van de kleuterschool.
translations.hi: मैं लड़के को किंडरगार्टन से लेने जा रहा हूँ।
translations.el: Παίρνω το αγόρι από το νηπιαγωγείο.
translations.id: Saya menjemput anak laki-laki itu dari taman kanak-kanak.
translations.ro: Îl iau pe băiat de la grădiniță.
translations.ja: 幼稚園に男の子を迎えに行きます。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun, 1st person singular, nominative; subject of the clause.
Finite verb, present indicative, 1st person singular; main predicate meaning ‘to fetch/pick up’.
Definite article, masculine accusative singular; marks the direct object.
Masculine noun; here accusative singular (weak -en form), the direct object of ‘holen’.
Preposition ‘von’ contracted with article ‘dem’ (von + dem → vom); governs dative; introduces source/origin.
Masculine noun; dative singular as object of ‘von’; denotes the institution (preschool).

translations.en: The boy is playing in the courtyard.
translations.es: El niño está jugando en el patio.
translations.fr: Le garçon joue dans la cour.
translations.pt: O menino está brincando no pátio.
translations.vi: Cậu bé đang chơi ở sân.
translations.nl: De jongen speelt op de binnenplaats.
translations.hi: लड़का आँगन में खेल रहा है।
translations.id: Anak laki-laki itu sedang bermain di halaman.
translations.ja: 男の子は中庭で遊んでいる。

tokens.explanation: Definite article, masculine nominative singular; marks the subject.
Masculine noun; nominative singular; subject of the sentence.
Finite verb, present indicative, 3rd person singular; main predicate.
Preposition ‘in’ contracted with ‘dem’ (in + dem → im); governs dative; introduces location.
Masculine noun; dative singular as object of ‘in’; place where the action occurs.

sentences.target: Heute kommt der Bub später nach Hause.
sentences.reading: /ˈhɔʏtə kɔmt deːɐ̯ buːp ˈʃpɛːtɐ naːx ˈhaʊzə./

translations.de: Heute kommt der Bub später nach Hause.
translations.ipa: /ˈhɔʏtə kɔmt deːɐ̯ buːp ˈʃpɛːtɐ naːx ˈhaʊzə./
translations.en: Today the boy is coming home later.
translations.es: Hoy el niño llega a casa más tarde.
translations.fr: Aujourd'hui, le garçon rentre plus tard.
translations.it: Oggi il bambino torna a casa più tardi.
translations.zh-Hans: 今天那个男孩会晚点回家。
translations.yue: 嗰個男仔今日會遲啲返屋企。
translations.pt: Hoje o menino vai chegar em casa mais tarde.
translations.he: היום הילד יחזור הביתה מאוחר יותר.
translations.tr: Bugün oğlan eve daha geç gelecek.
translations.vi: Hôm nay cậu bé sẽ về nhà muộn hơn.
translations.ar: اليوم سيعود الصبي إلى البيت متأخرًا.
translations.nl: Vandaag komt de jongen later thuis.
translations.hu: Ma a fiú később жön haza.
translations.hi: आज लड़का घर देर से आएगा।
translations.pl: Dziś chłopiec wróci do domu później.
translations.el: Σήμερα το αγόρι θα γυρίσει σπίτι αργότερα.
translations.nb: I dag kommer gutten hjem senere.
translations.id: Hari ini anak laki-laki itu pulang lebih lambat.
translations.sv: I dag kommer pojken hem senare.
translations.ro: Astăzi băiatul ajunge acasă mai târziu.
translations.da: I dag kommer drengen hjem senere.
translations.ja: 今日は男の子は遅く帰ってくる。

tokens.explanation: Temporal adverb; sets the time frame; modifies the clause.
Finite verb, present indicative, 3rd person singular; main predicate.
Definite article, masculine nominative singular; marks the subject.
Masculine noun; nominative singular; subject.
Comparative temporal adverb; modifies ‘kommt’ to indicate a later time.
Preposition; in the fixed expression ‘nach Hause’ indicating direction towards home; governs dative.
Neuter noun in fossilized dative singular form used in the fixed phrase ‘nach Hause’; denotes destination ‘home’.

sentences.target: Sein Vater wartet auf den Buben am Tor.
sentences.reading: /zaɪn ˈfaːtɐ ˈvaʁtət aʊf deːn ˈbuːbən am toːʁ./

translations.de: Sein Vater wartet auf den Buben am Tor.
translations.ipa: /zaɪn ˈfaːtɐ ˈvaʁtət aʊf deːn ˈbuːbən am toːʁ./
translations.en: His father is waiting for the boy at the gate.
translations.es: Su padre está esperando al niño en la puerta.
translations.fr: Son père attend le garçon à la grille.
translations.it: Suo padre aspetta il bambino al cancello.
translations.ko: 그의 아버지가 문 앞에서 남자아이를 기다리고 있어요.
translations.zh-Hans: 他的父亲在门口等着那个男孩。
translations.yue: 佢阿爸喺門口等緊個男仔。
translations.ru: Его отец ждёт мальчика у ворот.
translations.pt: O pai dele está esperando o menino no portão.
translations.vi: Bố của cậu ấy đang đợi cậu bé ở cổng.
translations.nl: Zijn vader wacht op de jongen bij het hek.
translations.uk: Його батько чекає хлопчика біля воріт.
translations.hu: Az apja a kapunál várja a fiút.
translations.hi: उसका पिता गेट पर लड़के का इंतज़ार कर रहा है।
translations.pl: Jego ojciec czeka na chłopca przy bramie.
translations.el: Ο πατέρας του περιμένει το αγόρι στην πύλη.
translations.nb: Faren hans venter på gutten ved porten.
translations.id: Ayahnya menunggu anak laki-laki itu di gerbang.
translations.sv: Hans pappa väntar på pojken vid grinden.
translations.ro: Tatăl lui îl așteaptă pe băiat la poartă.
translations.cs: Jeho otec čeká na kluka y brány.
translations.da: Hans far venter på drengen ved porten.
translations.ja: 彼の父親が門のところで男の子を待っている。

tokens.explanation: Possessive determiner; agrees with masculine singular noun; marks possession of ‘Vater’.
Masculine noun; nominative singular; subject of the sentence.
Finite verb, present indicative, 3rd person singular; main predicate.
Preposition used with ‘warten’ (‘warten auf’ = to wait for); selects accusative object.
Definite article, masculine accusative singular; marks the object of ‘auf’.
Masculine noun; accusative singular (weak -en form); object of the prepositional phrase ‘auf den Buben’.
Preposition ‘an’ contracted with ‘dem’ (an + dem → am); governs dative; introduces location.
Neuter noun; dative singular as object of ‘an’; indicates the place of waiting.
